responses: Hello! Welcome to JNTUH UCE Wanaparthy. How can I help you?
Hi! Ask me anything about UCEW college.
prathima, navya sri, rahul and bala raju created me.
my name is college Assit.
I'm college Assit.
The college name is JNTUH University College of Engineering, Wanaparthy (UCEW).

JNTUH UCE Wanaparthy is a constituent government engineering college established in 2022 by the Government of Telangana under JNTUH.It aims to provide quality technical education and develop competent professionals. The institute is temporarily functioning at the Palamuru University PG Centre, and a permanent campus is planned. The college serves students from Telangana and nearby regions with modern engineering programs.
JNTUH UCE Wanaparthy was established in the year 2022.

To emerge as a centre for excellence that contributes to sustainable development in technical education while developing the total personality of students into responsible citizens and competent professionals.
To achieve excellence in technical education and innovation.
To foster collaboration with industry and other academic institutions.
To provide affordable, quality education to meet industry and societal needs.
To create a conducive learning atmosphere that inspires creativity.
To instill high standards of ethics, values, and accountability in students..
Narsingaipalli Village, Gopalpet Road, Wanaparthy District, Telangana - 509103, India.

The B.Tech programs offered are Civil Engineering(CE), Mechanical Engineering(ME), Electronic & Communication Engineering(ECE),Computer Science & Engineering (CSE), and Computer Science & Engineering (Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning) (CSE(AI & ML)(CSM)).
Each branch has an intake of 60 students, with a total intake of 300 students.
Admissions are made through the state-level engineering entrance exam (e.g., TS EAPCET) followed by counseling and seat allotment as per the Government of Telangana rules.
Students must pass 10+2 with Mathematics, Physics, and Chemistry and qualify TS EAPCET.
The principal of UCEW is Dr. N. V. S. Raju.
The vice principal is Dr. B. V. Ram Naresh Yadav.
The college is temporarily functioning at Palamuru University PG Centre. A permanent campus is planned and almost ready.
Hostel facilities are available separately for boys and girls.Hostel bill be generated for number of days the student is staying in the hostel. The approximate hostel and mess fee is ₹4000.
Facilities include classrooms, laboratories, library, Wi-Fi, training and placement guidance, and workshops.
Examinations are typically conducted at the temporary campus in Wanaparthy.

How do I contact the college?
responses: You can contact UCEW at principal-jntuhucew@jntuh.ac.in or visit https://ucew.jntuh.ac.in
The college conducts workshops, seminars, orientation programs, employability training, and cultural events.
Top companies visit our campus every year and the college has a dedicated placement cell.
Tution fee is 50,000 plus special fee is added every year.
R18 CSE refers to the Computer Science and Engineering syllabus under JNTU Hyderabad regulations introduced in 2018.

R18 CSE 1st year subjects include Mathematics-I, Engineering Physics, Engineering Chemistry, Programming for Problem Solving, Engineering Drawing, English, Basic Electrical and Mechanical Engineering along with labs.

patterns: second year first semester r18 cse
responses: R18 CSE 2-1 subjects are Mathematics-III, Digital Logic Design, Data Structures, Computer Organization, Object Oriented Programming (Java) and related labs.

patterns: second year second semester r18 cse
responses: R18 CSE 2-2 subjects include Probability and Statistics, DBMS, Operating Systems, FLAT, Software Engineering and labs.

patterns: third year first semester r18 cse
responses: R18 CSE 3-1 subjects are Compiler Design, Computer Networks, Design and Analysis of Algorithms, Web Technologies, and one elective.

patterns: third year second semester r18 cse
responses: R18 CSE 3-2 subjects include Machine Learning, Data Mining, Cryptography and Network Security along with electives and labs.
R18 CSE final year includes Cloud Computing, Big Data Analytics, electives, Project Phase-I, Project Phase-II and Seminar.
R18 CSE follows a credit-based system with 70% external examination and 30% internal assessment.
R18 CSM stands for Computer Science and Engineering with specialization in Machine Learning, introduced by JNTU Hyderabad in 2018.

R18 CSM first year is common for all branches and includes Mathematics-I, Engineering Physics, Engineering Chemistry, Programming for Problem Solving, Engineering Drawing, English, and Basic Electrical & Mechanical Engineering.

patterns: second year first semester csm r18
responses: R18 CSM 2-1 subjects include Mathematics-III, Data Structures, Digital Logic Design, Computer Organization, and Object Oriented Programming using Java.

patterns: second year second semester csm r18
responses: R18 CSM 2-2 subjects include Probability and Statistics, Database Management Systems, Operating Systems, Software Engineering, and Automata Theory.

patterns: third year first semester csm r18
responses: R18 CSM 3-1 subjects include Machine Learning, Design and Analysis of Algorithms, Computer Networks, Web Technologies, and one professional elective.

patterns: third year second semester csm r18
responses: R18 CSM 3-2 subjects include Artificial Intelligence, Data Mining, Cryptography and Network Security, along with professional electives and labs.
R18 CSM final year includes Cloud Computing, Big Data Analytics, professional electives, Project Phase-I, Project Phase-II, and Seminar.
R18 CSM follows a credit-based evaluation system with 70 percent external examination and 30 percent internal assessment.
CSM graduates can work as Machine Learning Engineers, Data Scientists, AI Engineers, Software Developers, or pursue higher studies like M.Tech or MS.
R18 ECE refers to the Electronics and Communication Engineering syllabus under JNTU Hyderabad regulations introduced in 2018.

R18 ECE first year is common for all branches and includes Mathematics-I, Engineering Physics, Engineering Chemistry, Programming for Problem Solving, Engineering Drawing, English, and Basic Electrical & Mechanical Engineering.

patterns: second year first semester ece r18
responses: R18 ECE 2-1 subjects include Mathematics-III, Network Theory, Electronic Devices and Circuits, Digital Logic Design, Signals and Systems, along with related labs.

patterns: second year second semester ece r18
responses: R18 ECE 2-2 subjects include Probability and Random Processes, Analog Communications, Linear IC Applications, Control Systems, and labs.

patterns: third year first semester ece r18
responses: R18 ECE 3-1 subjects include Digital Communications, Microprocessors and Microcontrollers, Electromagnetic Fields, Computer Networks, and one professional elective.

patterns: third year second semester ece r18
responses: R18 ECE 3-2 subjects include VLSI Design, Embedded Systems, Antennas and Wave Propagation, Optical Communications, along with electives and labs.
R18 ECE final year includes professional electives, Open Elective, Project Phase-I, Project Phase-II, and Seminar.
R18 ECE follows a credit-based evaluation system with 70 percent external examination and 30 percent internal assessment.
ECE graduates can work as Electronics Engineers, Communication Engineers, VLSI Designers, Embedded Systems Developers, Network Engineers, or pursue higher studies.
R18 Civil Engineering refers to the Civil Engineering syllabus under JNTU Hyderabad regulations introduced in 2018.

R18 Civil first year is common for all branches and includes Mathematics-I, Engineering Physics, Engineering Chemistry, Programming for Problem Solving, Engineering Drawing, English, and Basic Electrical & Mechanical Engineering.

patterns: second year first semester civil r18
responses: R18 Civil 2-1 subjects include Mathematics-III, Engineering Mechanics, Strength of Materials, Surveying, and Building Materials & Construction.

patterns: second year second semester civil r18
responses: R18 Civil 2-2 subjects include Structural Analysis, Concrete Technology, Geotechnical Engineering-I, Environmental Engineering-I, and labs.

patterns: third year first semester civil r18
responses: R18 Civil 3-1 subjects include Design of Reinforced Concrete Structures, Geotechnical Engineering-II, Transportation Engineering-I, and Water Resources Engineering-I.

patterns: third year second semester civil r18
responses: R18 Civil 3-2 subjects include Structural Steel Design, Transportation Engineering-II, Environmental Engineering-II, Water Resources Engineering-II, and electives.
R18 Civil final year includes professional electives, Open Elective, Project Phase-I, Project Phase-II, and Seminar.
R18 Civil follows a credit-based evaluation system with 70 percent external examination and 30 percent internal assessment.
Civil Engineering graduates can work as Site Engineers, Structural Engineers, Planning Engineers, Government Engineers, or pursue higher studies like M.Tech or competitive exams.
R18 Mechanical Engineering refers to the Mechanical Engineering syllabus under JNTU Hyderabad regulations introduced in 2018.

R18 Mechanical first year is common for all branches and includes Mathematics-I, Engineering Physics, Engineering Chemistry, Programming for Problem Solving, Engineering Drawing, English, and Basic Electrical & Mechanical Engineering.

R18 Mechanical follows a credit-based evaluation system with 70 percent external examination and 30 percent internal assessment.
Mechanical Engineering graduates can work as Design Engineers, Production Engineers, Quality Engineers, Maintenance Engineers, or pursue higher studies and government exams.
R22 CSE refers to the Computer Science and Engineering syllabus under JNTU Hyderabad regulations introduced in 2022.
R22 CSE first year includes Mathematics-I, Applied Physics, Applied Chemistry, Programming for Problem Solving, Engineering Graphics, English, and Basic Electrical & Mechanical Engineering with labs.

patterns: second year first semester cse r22
responses: R22 CSE 2-1 subjects include Discrete Mathematics, Data Structures, Digital Logic Design, Computer Organization, and Object Oriented Programming using Java.

patterns: second year second semester cse r22

patterns: third year first semester cse r22
responses: R22 CSE 3-1 subjects include Design and Analysis of Algorithms, Computer Networks, Web Technologies, Artificial Intelligence, and one professional elective.

patterns: third year second semester cse r22

R22 CSE final year includes professional electives, open elective, Project Phase-I, Project Phase-II, and internship or seminar.
R22 CSE follows an outcome-based education system with 70 percent external examination and 30 percent internal assessment.
R22 CSE graduates can work as Software Engineers, Data Scientists, AI Engineers, Cloud Engineers, Cyber Security Analysts, or pursue higher studies like M.Tech, MS, or MBA.

R25 CSE refers to the Computer Science and Engineering program under JNTU Hyderabad regulations introduced in 2025, focusing on industry-oriented and skill-based learning.
R25 CSE first year includes Mathematics, Applied Physics, Applied Chemistry, Programming for Problem Solving, Engineering Graphics, Communication Skills, and basic engineering labs.

R25 CSE second year focuses on core computer science subjects such as Data Structures, Discrete Mathematics, Digital Logic Design, Computer Organization, Object Oriented Programming, DBMS, and Operating Systems.

R25 CSE third year includes advanced subjects like Design and Analysis of Algorithms, Computer Networks, Software Engineering, Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Web Technologies, and professional electives.
R25 CSE final year includes advanced electives, mandatory internship, major project work, seminar, and skill-based certifications.
R25 CSE follows an outcome-based education system with continuous internal assessment, end-semester examinations, and greater emphasis on projects and internships.

R25 CSE graduates can pursue careers as Software Engineers, Full Stack Developers, Data Scientists, AI Engineers, Cloud Engineers, Cyber Security Analysts, or opt for higher studies and entrepreneurship.
R25 CSM refers to Computer Science and Engineering with specialization in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning under JNTU Hyderabad regulations introduced in 2025.
R25 CSM first year includes Mathematics, Applied Physics, Applied Chemistry, Programming for Problem Solving, Engineering Graphics, Communication Skills, and basic engineering labs.

R25 CSM second year focuses on core computer science subjects such as Data Structures, Discrete Mathematics, Digital Logic Design, Computer Organization, Object Oriented Programming, Database Management Systems, and Operating Systems.
R25 CSM third year includes Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Design and Analysis of Algorithms, Computer Networks, Web Technologies, Data Science, and professional electives.
R25 CSM final year includes advanced AI and ML electives, mandatory internship, major project work, seminar, and industry-oriented certifications.
R25 CSM follows an outcome-based education system with continuous internal assessment, end-semester examinations, and strong emphasis on projects, internships, and skill development.
R25 CSM graduates can work as AI Engineers, Machine Learning Engineers, Data Scientists, Software Developers, Cloud Engineers, or pursue higher studies, research, and entrepreneurship.
R25 ECE refers to the Electronics and Communication Engineering program under JNTU Hyderabad regulations introduced in 2025, focusing on modern communication and electronics technologies.
R25 ECE first year includes Mathematics, Applied Physics, Applied Chemistry, Programming for Problem Solving, Engineering Graphics, Communication Skills, and basic engineering labs.

R25 ECE second year covers core subjects such as Network Theory, Electronic Devices and Circuits, Digital Logic Design, Signals and Systems, Linear Integrated Circuits, Control Systems, and related labs.
R25 ECE third year includes Digital Communications, Microprocessors & Microcontrollers, Electromagnetic Fields, VLSI Design, Embedded Systems, Optical Communication, and professional electives.
R25 ECE final year includes advanced electives, open elective, mandatory internship, Project Phase-I, Project Phase-II, seminar, and industry-oriented certifications.
R25 ECE follows an outcome-based education system with continuous internal assessment, end-semester examinations, and emphasis on projects and internships.
R25 ECE graduates can work as Electronics Engineers, Communication Engineers, Embedded Systems Engineers, VLSI Designers, Network Engineers, or pursue higher studies and research opportunities.
R25 Civil Engineering refers to the Civil Engineering program under JNTU Hyderabad regulations introduced in 2025, focusing on modern construction techniques and infrastructure development.
R25 Civil first year includes Mathematics, Applied Physics, Applied Chemistry, Programming for Problem Solving, Engineering Graphics, Communication Skills, and basic engineering labs.
R25 Civil second year covers Engineering Mechanics, Strength of Materials, Surveying, Building Materials and Construction, and Geotechnical Engineering-I.

R25 Civil third year includes Structural Analysis, Design of Reinforced Concrete Structures, Geotechnical Engineering-II, Transportation Engineering, Water Resources Engineering, and professional electives.
R25 Civil final year includes advanced professional electives, open elective, Project Phase-I, Project Phase-II, seminar, and mandatory internship.
R25 Civil follows an outcome-based education system with continuous internal assessment, end-semester examinations, and strong emphasis on projects and internships.

patterns: future scope of r25 civil engineering
responses: R25 Civil graduates can work as Site Engineers, Structural Engineers, Planning Engineers, Quality Engineers, Government Engineers, or pursue higher studies like M.Tech, GATE, or competitive exams.
R25 Mechanical Engineering refers to the Mechanical Engineering program under JNTU Hyderabad regulations introduced in 2025, focusing on industry-oriented and skill-based learning.
R25 Mechanical first year includes Mathematics, Applied Physics, Applied Chemistry, Programming for Problem Solving, Engineering Graphics, Communication Skills, and basic engineering labs.

R25 Mechanical second year covers core subjects such as Engineering Mechanics, Strength of Materials, Thermodynamics, Manufacturing Processes, Fluid Mechanics, and Electrical & Electronics Engineering.
R25 Mechanical third year includes advanced subjects like Heat Transfer, Design of Machine Elements, Dynamics of Machinery, CAD/CAM, Metrology, and professional electives.
R25 Mechanical final year includes advanced electives, mandatory internship, major project work, seminar, and industry-oriented skill certifications.
R25 Mechanical follows an outcome-based education system with continuous internal assessment, end-semester examinations, and strong emphasis on projects and internships.

patterns: future scope of r25 mechanical engineering
responses: R25 Mechanical Engineering graduates can work as Design Engineers, Production Engineers, Quality Engineers, Maintenance Engineers, Automobile Engineers, or pursue higher studies, research, and government exams.
